name: Las Palas y La Pinilla desde Mazarrón — return to the Sierra de Algaborro
name_original: Las Palas y La Pinilla desde Mazarrón — vuelta a la Sierra de Algaborro
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mazarrón offer a diverse landscape for cyclists, blending coastal roads with mountainous inland terrain. The region features a 35-kilometer coastline with fine sandy beaches and coves, alongside significant mountain ranges such as the Sierra de las Moreras and La Muela. Unique geological formations like the Bolnuevo Erosions provide distinctive visual elements along certain routes. This varied topography ensures a range of cycling experiences from scenic coastal rides to more challenging climbs. Best road cycling routes around Mazarrón The most popular road cycling route is Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations – Castellar Beach loop from Mazarrón, a 25.9 miles (41.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This route passes by the unique Bolnuevo Erosions and offers coastal views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castellar Beach – Cedacero Viewpoint loop from Puerto de Mazarrón, a moderate 43.7 miles (70.4 km) path. This route includes a viewpoint offering panoramic coastal vistas. Local road cyclists also love the Leiva, Atalaya and Morata through the Sierra de las Morenas — return from Puerto de Mazarrón, a 34.9 miles (56.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra de las Moreras, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Mazarrón is defined by coastal roads, mountainous terrain, and unique geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal loops to more challenging climbs in the Sierras. The routes in Mazarrón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mazarrón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of road cycling routes can I expect in Mazarrón?
A: Mazarrón offers a diverse range of road cycling experiences, blending scenic coastal roads with more challenging climbs through mountainous inland terrain. You'll find routes that traverse the 35-kilometer coastline, featuring fine sandy beaches and coves, as well as paths through significant mountain ranges like the Sierra de las Moreras and La Muela. Unique geological formations, such as the Bolnuevo Erosions, also provide distinctive visual elements along certain routes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Mazarrón?
A: Yes, Mazarrón offers routes suitable for various skill levels. For an easier experience, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27632057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations – Castellar Beach loop from Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated as easy and passes by the unique Bolnuevo Erosions. The &lt;a href="https://visitamazarron.com/en/mazarron/nature-and-landscapes/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;, a greenway developed along a former railway line, also provides a flat and accessible option for on-road bicycles, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For those seeking more challenging climbs, Mazarrón's inland mountain ranges offer excellent options. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009644543/leiva-atalaya-and-morata-through-the-sierra-de-las-morenas-return-from-puerto-de-mazarron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiva, Atalaya and Morata through the Sierra de las Moreras — return from Puerto de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;, with over 598 meters of elevation gain, provide a good test. Another moderate option with substantial climbing is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21867205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar Beach – Cedacero Viewpoint loop from Puerto de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 800 meters of ascent and panoramic coastal views.

Q: What unique natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes in Mazarrón?
A: Mazarrón is home to several unique natural attractions. The most famous are the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/998557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations&lt;/a&gt;, impressive 'mushroom-shaped' geological sculptures carved by wind and water. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27632057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations – Castellar Beach loop&lt;/a&gt;, pass directly by these natural monuments. You can also enjoy views of the diverse coastline with its unspoiled coves and the dramatic mountain ranges like the Sierra de las Moreras.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the Mazarrón area?
A: Yes, Mazarrón offers several longer road cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21867205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar Beach – Cedacero Viewpoint loop from Puerto de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 70.4 km (43.7 miles) path, providing a substantial ride with coastal vistas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009644543/leiva-atalaya-and-morata-through-the-sierra-de-las-morenas-return-from-puerto-de-mazarron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiva, Atalaya and Morata through the Sierra de las Moreras&lt;/a&gt; route, covering 56.2 km (34.9 miles) through varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mazarrón?
A: Mazarrón boasts an exceptionally favorable climate for road cycling, with around 300 days of sunshine per year and an average temperature of 20ºC. This makes it an ideal destination for year-round outdoor activities. While cycling is possible almost any time, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying abundant sunshine.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Mazarrón?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Mazarrón are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27632057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations – Castellar Beach loop from Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21867205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar Beach – Cedacero Viewpoint loop from Puerto de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes are convenient for exploring the region without needing to retrace your steps.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Mazarrón?
A: The road cycling routes in Mazarrón are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's varied terrain, which offers everything from scenic coastal rides to challenging climbs, and the unique natural landmarks like the Bolnuevo Erosions. Over 3,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mazarrón's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21867205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar Beach – Cedacero Viewpoint loop from Puerto de Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes a viewpoint offering panoramic coastal vistas. Additionally, routes through the Sierra de las Moreras and La Muela often provide stunning Mediterranean views from higher elevations. You can also find specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loma de las Lagunas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1005722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Las Antenas (Cerro de los Siete Cucones)&lt;/a&gt; listed as highlights in the area.

Q: What is the Vía Verde de Mazarrón and is it suitable for road bikes?
A: The Vía Verde de Mazarrón is a greenway developed along a former railway line, making it an excellent option for cyclists. The first section, which is 14 kilometers long, runs north from Mazarrón towards La Pinilla and is indeed suitable for on-road bicycles. It's a great choice for a relaxed ride away from traffic, and there are plans to extend it further south to Puerto de Mazarrón.

Q: Are there routes that explore the inland areas and mountains?
A: Yes, many routes venture into the mountainous interior of Mazarrón. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010622299/las-palas-y-la-pinilla-desde-mazarron-return-to-the-sierra-de-algaborro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Palas y La Pinilla desde Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt; route takes you through the Sierra de Algaborro. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010201361/la-pinilla-via-el-saladillo-and-return-via-las-ventas-return-from-mazarron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pinilla via El Saladillo and return via Las Ventas&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the inland landscapes around La Pinilla, offering a blend of countryside and mountain scenery.
tips: Beautiful views next to the Alamillo beach, a transit area of the EuroVelo Mediterranean route. A delight for lovers of two wheels to be able to ride so close to the sea.

The best thing is not to go on weekends (like me), because of the adrenaline rush, some of the numerous motorcyclists seem to be floating around headless on the winding roads ... during the week, the scenery is guaranteed to be much better enjoyed.

Partly very bad tarmac (broken, chippings, some bad potholes) but a fantastic breathtaking view and every pothole worth. The slope gradient is not so sheer (I guess max 10%, more less).

Partly very bad asphalt (broken, a lot of grit, nasty potholes but a breathtaking view that is worth every pothole. The incline is not that bad, I'd guess a maximum of 10%, rather less.

waypoints.name: E19 Road Climb to La Pinilla
